fix_under_test: axolotl feature/expert-store @ dcd9ac2 (zero-decode mask of un-routed rows); build gated on _zero_decode_byte present
host: RunPod SECURE PRO 6000 Blackwell WS (sm_120), pod l6bmb14xbo3mxv, driver 610
body: Qwen3-30B-A3B seq256, sample_packing, seed 42, 150 steps, eval_steps 50; prediction_loss_only

ga4_floor.note: 5th consecutive clean warm floor; mean reproduces the decisive leg's 1.2623 on a fresh pod

efficacy.step0_eval: 2.902 == whole 2.902 (forward mask bit-identical, as proven); gap appears by step50 and holds

positive_control.verdict: VALID (>=100x void-gate); reproduces the decisive leg's 0.186
positive_control.note: mask cuts the legacy gap 0.1823 -> 0.1043 (~1.75x), same-bracket, real signal not noise

A4_attenuation.readout: DOES NOT ATTENUATE (0.916 ~ 1.0, far from 0.71) -> the divergence is a COHERENT/COMPOUNDING bias, NOT independent per-row noise. Doubling gradient averaging (ga4->ga8) barely moved the gap.
A4_attenuation.corroboration: consistent with the seq512 CONVEXITY (which already argued against the sqrt-quadrature/independent-noise story); two independent lines (dose-response curvature + gradient-averaging) now agree the mechanism is coherent.

synthesis.conclusion: The mask fix is CORRECT (un-routed rows -> exact 0.0, proven on real bnb) but INSUFFICIENT: it removes ~44% of the divergence (the fill-CONTENT component) and leaves a ~104x-floor coherent residual. Content was never the whole story.
synthesis.residual_mechanism_hypothesis: routing DRIFT across the gradient-checkpoint recompute: as the attention LoRA trains, recomputed hidden states route some tokens to experts OUTSIDE the originally-staged union; those read a zeroed row instead of the correct expert -> a coherent, systematic (not random) error that gradient averaging cannot cancel and that GROWS with fill (fewer staged experts -> more drift-exposed tokens). Explains BOTH the 1.75x-not-full reduction AND the non-attenuation AND the convexity.
synthesis.consequence: routed-subset TRAINING remains non-viable; the fix is a partial mitigation, not a solution. Forward/decode stays bit-identical and unaffected. whole_layer is still the only training-validated staging.
synthesis.next_step_options: (a) test the drift hypothesis directly: log the recompute routed-set vs the forward routed-set overlap (cheap instrumentation, one short run); (b) drift-robust staging = stage a superset (union over a few recent forwards, or top-k with margin) -> trades bandwidth for correctness, converges toward whole_layer as drift grows; (c) re-derive the routed set at recompute time from the recompute's own routing (requires the stager to see the recompute forward).
